meta.purpose: Define practice stages, capability layers, and measurable growth indicators for code review skill development.
meta.load_condition: Load when the user asks how to practice, improve, measure progress, or identify current level.

stages.name: Silent Reader
stages.description: Reads code changes but rarely comments. When they do comment, feedback is vague ('looks good', 'maybe refactor this'). Does not classify comments or distinguish blocking from non-blocking.
indicators: Leaves fewer than 2 substantive comments per review on average
Cannot name the primary risk in a change before being asked
Uses vague language: 'clean this up', 'improve this', 'this feels wrong'
Approves PRs without articulating what was checked

stages.name: Pattern Matcher
stages.description: Identifies known patterns and applies standard rules. Catches surface-level issues reliably but struggles with novel problems or architectural tradeoffs. Comments are specific but may lack prioritization.
indicators: Catches style issues, obvious bugs, and known anti-patterns reliably
Can classify comments but defaults most to non-blocking
Rarely identifies architectural risks or coupling before they cause problems
Review comments address the code, not the intent or context

stages.name: Risk-Aware Reviewer
stages.description: Evaluates changes through a lens of risk and impact. Prioritizes by what could go wrong. Reads intent before code. Classifies every comment deliberately. Feedback shapes not just the code but the author's thinking.
indicators: Starts every review by identifying the highest-risk change
Blocks on security, data integrity, and irreversible complexity
Review comments teach principles, not just flag problems
Can explain why a seemingly-working design will fail under future conditions

stages.name: System Builder
stages.description: Designs review processes and automation that improve the entire team's review quality. Teaches others to become risk-aware reviewers. The team's review velocity and quality no longer depend on any single reviewer.
indicators: Review checklists and automated checks catch the issues they used to catch manually
Junior developers' reviews show risk-aware patterns within their first quarter
Post-incident reviews never reveal issues that should have been caught in code review
Team's average PR resolution time is under 24 hours

evolution_layers.name: Comment Specificity
evolution_layers.capability: Ability to write comments that name the specific issue, its impact, and an actionable fix direction.

evolution_layers.name: Intent Reading
evolution_layers.capability: Ability to evaluate code based on the problem it solves and the constraints it faces, not just surface correctness.

evolution_layers.name: Review System Design
evolution_layers.capability: Ability to build processes, tools, and training that produce better reviews across the entire team.

measurement.what: Percentage of review comments that carry explicit classification
measurement.how: Sample 20 reviews from the team. Count comments with and without [blocking]/[non-blocking]/[nitpick]/[question] prefixes.
measurement.threshold: At least 90% of comments are classified.

measurement.what: Whether blocking issues appear before non-blocking comments in the review
measurement.how: Check if the first comment in the review addresses the highest-risk concern. In 20 sampled reviews, verify ordering.
measurement.threshold: In at least 80% of reviews, the first comment addresses the highest-risk issue.

measurement.what: Time from PR submission to first meaningful review feedback
measurement.how: Measure median time from PR open to first classified review comment.

measurement.what: Production issues traceable to gaps in code review
measurement.how: For each post-release incident or bug, classify whether the root cause was reviewable and whether it was missed in review.
measurement.threshold: Zero incidents per quarter where a reviewable issue was missed due to review quality.
